summary: Frontend-only regression for the new 'Databases' feature + Change Logs 'Optimized' category + Update modal. Login works, sidebar shows nav-databases directly after nav-projects, /databases page renders with dbtest row (dbtest_db, 215 B, 1 collection, Provisioned, staging env badge). End-to-end backup, manage-backups, restore-with-drop-overwrite, and delete-backup flows all work with real mongodump/mongorestore in the sandbox (backup + restore both returned a green success result via db-job-result and disappeared from the manage list after delete). Update modal renders with update-cancel + update-confirm and closes cleanly. Navigation across Overview/Projects/Databases/Terminal/Activity/Settings produced ZERO console errors. Found ONE HIGH-priority bug: the Change Logs modal's category filter does NOT show an 'Optimized' option — the current [Unreleased] section (which contains the only 'Optimized' entries) is not parsed by /api/system/changelog, so both the Optimized filter option and the new Unreleased entries are invisible to users.

critical.issue: Parser regex rel_re only matches releases with the pattern '## [x.y.z] — YYYY-MM …'. The '## [Unreleased]' block in /app/CHANGELOG.md (which is the ONLY block containing an '### Optimized' section) is silently skipped. Result: (a) the ChangelogModal filter dynamically built from returned section types has no 'Optimized' option (only All / Added / Changed / Fixed), and (b) users never see the new Unreleased entries about Databases + streaming Update flow. Verified via curl on the deployed API: Counter({'Added': 31, 'Changed': 5, 'Fixed': 3}) — zero 'Optimized' entries returned.

ui_bugs.issue: Filter option 'Optimized' missing because backend never emits an Optimized section (see backend bug). No frontend fix needed once backend parser is corrected — SECTION_META already maps Optimized -> Gauge icon.

action_items: Fix /api/system/changelog parser in /app/backend/server.py (~line 891): extend rel_re (or add a second branch) so '## [Unreleased]' is captured as its own release entry (e.g., version='Unreleased', date=''). Alternatively, allow the date group to be optional. Once fixed, the ChangelogModal filter will auto-list 'Optimized' since the frontend derives categories from returned sections.
(Optional) Consider showing the Unreleased entry at the top of the modal with a distinct 'Preview' badge instead of the 'New Version' pill.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py system_changelog(): rel_re requires a date component after the version — this hard-drops the entire '[Unreleased]' block, hiding the newest work from users. Regex should treat the date as optional and the parser should not depend on the '—' separator being present.

The changelog filter categories are derived only from what the API returns (ChangelogModal.jsx line 29-33). This is fine, but it means any parser drop silently removes UI capabilities — worth adding a static superset of known section types as a fallback so the filter UX stays predictable.

success_rate.frontend: 89% (8/9 requested checks passed; changelog Optimized filter failed)

seed_data_creation: Created & then deleted one dbtest backup archive (dbtest_db__20260722-132043.archive.gz) as part of the backup→restore→delete flow. No lingering test data (delete verified — list is empty afterwards).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Databases page + backup/restore/delete flows are solid. When retesting after the changelog parser fix, open the Change Logs modal ([data-testid=panel-version]) and confirm the [data-testid=changelog-filter] contains an 'Optimized' option AND that selecting it renders the Unreleased entries (Databases + streaming Update flow) without console errors. Sandbox notes still apply: Docker OFF; /api/ops/update returns 400 (Update modal renders but Start update fails — expected).
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: /app/backend/server.py:891 rel_re = ^##\s*\[([^\]]+)\]\s*[—\-–]+\s*([0-9][0-9\-]*)\s*(?:·\s*(.*))?$ — the second capturing group requires a numeric date immediately after an em/en dash. The '## [Unreleased]' header in /app/CHANGELOG.md has no date, so the line does not match and cur_rel stays None; every subsequent '### Optimized' + '- ...' bullet is discarded. REPRODUCTION: (1) login and open Change Logs from navbar — filter dropdown shows only All/Added/Changed/Fixed (no Optimized), (2) curl /api/system/changelog with Bearer token — response has releases 1.0.0-1.4.0 only, no 'Unreleased' entry, section-type counter has zero Optimized items. MITIGATION: make the date group optional in rel_re, e.g. r'^##\s*\[([^\]]+)\](?:\s*[—\-–]+\s*([0-9][0-9\-]*))?\s*(?:·\s*(.*))?$', and default date to ''. That single-line fix restores the Optimized filter and the Unreleased entries.
